title: Step 5: Teach points
description:
Step 5: Teach points

Step 6: Teach points

The camera must be aligned with the Z axis of the local coordinate system.  When using 6-axis robots, normally the V coordinate for camera view points will be 0 and the W coordinate will be 0 or 180, depending on the orientation of the local.  It is recommended that you align the camera to the local coordinate system before teaching points.  You normally should not need to change V and W when teaching the calibration points.  This keeps the camera in the same alignment relative to the local for each calibration view position.

On Vision Guide Calibration tab, click the Teach Points button.  This will open the Teach Calibration Points window.
Follow the instructions displayed in the message box at the bottom of the dialog.
If you are using an upward camera to find the reference point, then skip the next step.
Teach the reference point.  This means the robot must be positioned properly such that the tool being used to get the part is positioned directly over the part. (Just like if you were teaching this position for the robot to move to.)
Teach the camera points.  There are nine camera positions to be taught.  These represent 9 positions of the robot such that the 1st position is seen in the upper left corner of the image display, the 2nd position in the upper center of the image display and so on.  For best results, these points should span the field of view of the camera.
